latestPosts.caption: She’s here 📖🥹 type “BOOK” to pre-order today (!!!)

I AM IN SHOCK!!! Thank you @nytimes !!!!! 

The Secret Language of Work is officially a NYT BESTSELLER 🥹

@Forbes 30 Under 30 class of 2025 🥹

4 years ago (almost to the day), I sat in my cozy 400sqft Brooklyn apartment, bored out of my mind as the pandemic stalled all my exciting work in the film industry + I was living on my emergency fund. 

As I scrolled TikTok, I thought about what I could do to make the world a tiny bit better. How could I help people feel happier and more confident? I thought it would be cool to post some career and life advice, things I would tell my younger self—but make it realistic, super helpful, and relatable lol. 

So, I propped my phone up on my AC unit and recorded “how to answer the worst interview question ever: tell me about yourself”. I edited it, posted it, and went to bed. I woke up with over 20M views on that video and thousands of DMs flooding my account. 

I saw the impact just one little video made on so many lives. I became addicted to the challenge of making education (esp such “boring” topics like how to make a resume or save for retirement) fun + exciting + accessible. 

Since then, we’ve grown to a community of 6.5 MILLION people from all over the world. Hundreds of thousands have used my templates to advocate for themselves, negotiate higher pay for them + the ones they care for, and land their dream jobs.

So THANK YOU @forbes & congrats to the class of 2025. 

And thank you, whoever you are, wherever you are reading this, for supporting me + my mission by being here. 

+ lastly, thank you to my husband @michaelponton who supports me every single day & is the best life collaborator a girl could wish for. 

#ForbesUnder30
Baby Jack is here! 🍼✨ the third of the triplets has been born iykyk 😉

Hiiii 👋 just your freshly postpartum friendly neighborhood career-oriented girl who was apprehensive about pregnancy and scared of childbirth checking in to share her positive experience!!! 10/10 recommend - on cloud 9! ☁️ 

Am I randomly weeping? Yes. 
Does my body hurt? Yes. 
Am I anxious about the next few months? Yes. 
But it’s all figure-out-able — especially with the right partner + prep 🤍🤍🤍

ITS HAPPENING!! 

The ironic thing is we are actually VERY zen rn lol have the lights off and soft music playing… I just took a nap!! But couldn’t pass up a chance to use The Office sound 🤪

See you on the other side! I got this ?? 🤞🏼

Steal these scripts to impress any recruiter!! 👀💬 #jobinterview #careeradvice 

Behavioral job interview questions = professional storytimes 💤📘 — don’t focus on details, focus on telling a good story!! 

Have these 6 stories prepared - they fit almost any prompt! 💬 

PS - type “FISH” to try my new app, StupidFish, which provides 24/7 career advice, created by my team! 

💌 Remember to save this post 👀 

[Story]
At eighteen I was elected mayor of my small town, and my big initiative was building a winter sports complex called Ice Town.

[Take Ownership]
I massively underestimated the costs, the rink hemorrhaged money, and I bankrupted the town in under a year. That one’s on me — I was overconfident and didn’t do the financial diligence the project needed.

[Learnings]
It’s why I became obsessive about budgets and numbers. In every role since, I’m the person who catches the line item that doesn’t add up before it becomes a problem. That failure is the whole reason I’m good at this now!

[Context]
When I took over the Scranton branch, corporate had it on the list of locations to shut down.

[How You Stepped Up]
I made a choice to lead with relationships — I learned what motivated each person, protected my team from layoffs whenever I could, and sold alongside them instead of barking quotas from an office.

[Result]
Scranton went from the chopping block to the most profitable branch in the company. When corporate consolidated, they closed the other branch and moved those people to us. I’m really proud that I kept my whole team employed and turned the branch around!

EXAMPLE: 

[Disagreement] 
My coworker Ron and I hit a wall when I wanted to turn an abandoned lot into a community park and he wanted to leave it empty because it ‘cost taxpayers zero dollars.’ 

[What You Did] 
Instead of steamrolling him, I figured out what he actually cared about — not wasting money or people’s time — and rebuilt my pitch around that. I lined up private sponsors, kept it under budget, and brought him the data on how parks raise property-tax revenue. 

[Result] 
He approved it, the park became the most-used spot in Pawnee. I’m really proud of how I was able to navigate that toug

Don’t leave your career to chance! These three book are the best selling and highest rated to give you the competitive edge you need!

📖 CLOCK IN: No-BS career advice to help you get ahead. Resume advice, insider tips on networking? And how to land a great job.
 

📖GET IT IN WRITING: The ultimate guide to your rights at work, includes checklists and sample emails written by a real workers rights lawyer. Protect your job, your money, and your sanity with this guide!

📖 THE SECRET LANGUAGE OF WORK: Hyper-helpful scripts for every day situations. Handle awkward moments and high stakes conversations with confidence.

GET ALL THREE TO UNLOCK YOUR POTENTIAL!

Which book do you want to read first this summer? Drop a 📖 if you’ve already read at least one!

“Follow your passion” = worst career advice ever 👎

But no need to fear, AdviceWithErin is here!!! 😎

We actually kind of sort of invented an app that helps you find your “dream job” ✨

It’s called StupidFish & it helps you discover hidden career opportunities & matches you with ideal career paths 📲

Find your dream life & then a job that fits INTO that life. You got this 👏 #careeradvice 

Type “FISH” for the link & see for yourself!! 😍🫧🐟
Still pregnant !! Being on maternity leave with no baby yet is a very weird feeling lol

Today, I am SO excited to announce my brand new partnership with Forbes as a Forbes Creator! ✨

As the job market faces rapid change, people need help adapting. 

Forbes X AdviceWithErin are joining forces to create new and exciting ways to help professionals everywhere navigate this change—and thrive as a result. 💪 

I believe everyone can craft a career they love, and I’m honored that Forbes is giving me a bigger platform to allow my work to make an even deeper impact. 

We have some VERY exciting initiatives we’re cookin’ up. Stay tuned! 🍳 

PS — are you interested in very fun + chill live networking events? Let me know what city you’re in 👀

🍻 reposting my dads viral video from a couple years ago when we were 4 days deep to camping in Yosemite lol

Happy Father’s Day to the man who gives AdviceWithErin advice (and my sense of humor lol) — my dad!

And to all the dads out there who are working hard to be the best person they can be for their kids - you’re doing great! You got this 👏
